evidence_tier: B (reproducible, first-party mapping judgement against real documented vendor schemas and the real OCSF 1.8.0 schema; not production telemetry)

scope.okta: System Log LogEvent -> Authentication (3002); anchored on Okta's okta/okta-ocsf-syslog reference mapper where it maps
scope.crowdstrike: Detection Summary Event (Event Streams) -> Detection Finding (2004); best-effort vs OCSF 1.8.0 (no public vendor field mapping)
scope.note: Okta inventory is the documented typed schema; CrowdStrike inventory is the publicly-reproduced Detection Summary Event, NOT the gated full FDR schema. Coverage is over each scoped field set, not all telemetry.

fields.note: raw type string preserved in event_code; its semantics also drive class/activity selection
DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR -> severity_id enum; faithful enum remap
actor is the authenticating user for a login
OCSF has no parsed-browser attribute; only the raw UA has a home
geo carried (Okta puts it in enrichments; typed home is src_endpoint.location)
Okta network zone -> network_endpoint.zone; shipped mapper drops it
device label (Computer/Mobile) -> device.type; shipped mapper puts it in src_endpoint.interface_id, a coercion either way
proxy chain -> ip list; per-hop geo and ordering are lost; shipped mapper drops it
Authentication 3002 has no target/resource array; heterogeneous target[] (app/user/group) has no faithful home
WEB/JOB -> logon_type enum; shipped mapper maps WEB->99 (Other)
free-form map carrying risk/threat signals, deviceFingerprint, behaviors; shipped mapper extracts only requestUri+url, the rest lands in unmapped
provider enum -> auth_protocol enum; shipped mapper maps FACTOR->Other
RSA/DUO/YUBIKEY -> auth_factors[]; provider->factor mapping is lossy; shipped mapper drops it
12-value scheme enum (OTP/SMS/PASSWORD/PUSH...) collapses to is_mfa + factor_type; PUSH-vs-OTP distinction lost; shipped mapper drops it
issuer source info; no typed home
third-party UI string; no typed home
ASN -> network_endpoint.autonomous_system.number; shipped mapper drops the whole securityContext
AS org -> autonomous_system.name; shipped mapper drops it
OCSF endpoint has no boolean is-proxy flag (proxy_endpoint is an address, not a flag); high-signal fraud bit has no typed home
raw type preserved; semantics also drive class selection
CrowdStrike numeric severity scale -> OCSF severity_id enum; scale remap
tactic name string -> attack.tactic object {name,uid}; the TA-id is absent, only name populates
technique name/label -> attack.technique object {name,uid}; name and T-id do not both survive a flat string
adversary objective; no OCSF Detection Finding attribute
Falcon AID -> device.agent_list[].uid (the sensor, distinct from device identity)
OCSF process models one parent level + ancestry[]/lineage path; grandparent has no named slot, flattens into ancestry
ancestry/lineage carry path only, not a command line for ancestor processes; grandparent cmd line has no home
free string (hash_sha256/domain/ipv4) -> observable.type_id integer enum; IOC types outside the enum fail the lookup
bitmask of multiple actions taken -> single-valued disposition_id; the multi-action set collapses to one
named boolean flags object; no typed home
array of {RemoteAddress,...} -> evidences[]; inner structure only partially modeled
array of written executables w/ hashes -> evidences[]; partial
array of DNS objects -> evidences[]/observables[]; partial
AV scan results array; no clean Detection Finding home
reporting firewall serial -> device.uid (firewall as the observing device)
start/end/drop/deny -> activity_id enum; drop/deny are actions not lifecycle states, so they collapse
post-NAT source IP; Network Activity models ONE src_endpoint (the original) with no translated-address attribute, so the post-NAT address has no typed home [pre/post-NAT collapse]
post-NAT destination IP; no translated-address slot on dst_endpoint [pre/post-NAT collapse]
User-ID source user -> owner of the source endpoint
User-ID destination user -> owner of the destination endpoint
virtual-system partition id; no OCSF home
log-forwarding profile name (config metadata); no OCSF home
repeat count of same-5-tuple sessions -> count
post-NAT source port; no translated-port slot [pre/post-NAT collapse]
post-NAT destination port; no translated-port slot [pre/post-NAT collapse]
32-bit PA session-characteristics bitfield (NAT-applied, decrypted, proxy-session, captive-portal, pcap) — NOT TCP flags; high-signal bits like decrypted/NAT-applied have no OCSF home
allow/deny/drop/drop-icmp/reset-client/reset-server/reset-both/drop-all -> action_id (Allowed/Denied/...); the drop-vs-reset-vs-deny distinction collapses [firewall action-enum narrowing]

PA URL-category (single PANW-taxonomy value) -> url.categories (string preserved), but OCSF's typed url.category_ids enum uses a different category taxonomy, so an OCSF-category-id pivot loses the value [URL-category taxonomy seam]
Panorama log-forwarding bitfield (internal); no OCSF home
PA session-end-reason enum (tcp-fin/tcp-rst-from-client/tcp-rst-from-server/threat/policy-deny/aged-out/...) -> status_detail free string; OCSF Network Activity has no typed session-termination enum
named virtual system; no OCSF home
action provenance (from-policy / from-application); no OCSF home
tunnel encapsulation type (GRE/IPSec/GTP); Network Activity 4001 has no tunnel-type attribute
parent-tunnel session linkage for inner flows; no OCSF parent-session attribute [structure collapse]
single PA dynamic-user-group name -> source user's groups[]; PA's policy-evaluated dynamic membership flattens to a static group name
X-Forwarded-For true-client IP -> intermediate_ips; Network Activity has no http_request (only proxy_http_request), so the real client lands in the proxy-hop list and loses its 'true source' meaning
Device-ID source category -> endpoint type_id enum; the Device-ID category taxonomy and OCSF's endpoint-type enum do not align [device-id taxonomy]
granular Device-ID profile (e.g. 'Apple iPhone'); OCSF has no device-profile attribute
Device-ID destination category -> endpoint type_id enum; taxonomy mismatch [device-id taxonomy]
granular Device-ID destination profile; no OCSF device-profile attribute
source-IP External Dynamic List membership (threat-intel list name); OCSF endpoint has no list-membership attribute
destination-IP External Dynamic List membership; no OCSF home
source Dynamic Address Group membership; no OCSF home
destination Dynamic Address Group membership; no OCSF home
proxy vs non-proxy flow type; OCSF expresses proxy presence structurally (proxy_endpoint set), not as an enum, so the label has no home
App-ID subcategory; OCSF has no application-taxonomy attributes [app-id taxonomy seam]
App-ID category; no OCSF application-taxonomy home [app-id taxonomy seam]
App-ID technology (browser-based/client-server/network-protocol/peer-to-peer); no OCSF home [app-id taxonomy seam]
App-ID static application-risk rating (1-5); distinct from event risk_level_id (the event's risk, not the app's inherent rating), so no faithful home
App-ID characteristics (evasive/tunnels-other-apps/used-by-malware/...); no OCSF home [app-id taxonomy seam]
App-ID parent application; OCSF has no application-hierarchy attribute
tunneled application carried inside the parent app; no OCSF home [app-id taxonomy seam]
App-ID SaaS indicator; no OCSF home
App-ID sanctioned-SaaS indicator; no OCSF home
ASA hostname -> device.hostname (firewall as the observing device)
ASA/syslog severity 0-7 (8 levels) -> severity_id enum (~6 levels); 8->6 remap
Built/Teardown/Deny -> action_id (Allowed/Denied); Built and Teardown both collapse to Allowed, so the connection-lifecycle distinction OCSF would put in activity_id is lost from this single field
NAT-mapped source IP; Network Activity has no translated-address slot [pre/post-NAT collapse]
NAT-mapped source port; no translated-port slot [pre/post-NAT collapse]
NAT-mapped destination IP; no translated-address slot [pre/post-NAT collapse]
NAT-mapped destination port; no translated-port slot [pre/post-NAT collapse]
identity-firewall user -> owner of the source endpoint
total connection bytes -> traffic.bytes; ASA reports only a total (no directional bytes_in/out), so the total maps cleanly but the direction split PA carries is absent at the source
teardown reason enum (TCP FINs/TCP Reset/SYN Timeout/Connection timeout/...) -> status_detail free string; OCSF Network Activity has no typed session-termination enum
which side initiated the teardown; no OCSF attribute
ICMP type; OCSF Network Activity has no ICMP type/code attributes
ICMP code; OCSF Network Activity has no ICMP type/code attributes

the requesting identity is heterogeneous (roaming computer / AD user / network / site); flattening it to a single endpoint name loses the type-discriminated meaning that most_granular_identity_type carries [heterogeneous identity]
comma-list of ALL associated identities (device + user + network + AD groups at once); OCSF DNS Activity has no multi-identity list [structure collapse / heterogeneous]
egress/NAT public IP of the same requester; src_endpoint.ip already holds the internal client, and there is no second-address slot for the post-NAT egress [pre/post-NAT collapse]

Umbrella content/security categories matched by the domain (Umbrella taxonomy); OCSF DNS Activity has no domain-category attribute (url.categories is HTTP/url-bound, and DNS Activity has no url) [content-category taxonomy seam]

Umbrella identity-type (Roaming Computer/AD User/Network/Site/...) -> endpoint type_id enum; the taxonomy does not align, and types like 'AD User'/'Network'/'Site' are not endpoint types at all [identity-type taxonomy]
comma-list of the types of all associated identities; no OCSF home [structure collapse]
the categories that caused the block (Umbrella taxonomy); OCSF DNS Activity has no blocked-category/domain-category attribute [content-category taxonomy seam]
user login (email) -> owner of the source endpoint, email_addr
user department -> organization unit name; OCSF user has no dedicated department attribute, so it maps to the org-unit field
Zscaler gateway location / sub-location is a topology/policy object name, not geo; mapping it to src_endpoint.location (a geo object) would be wrong, and there is no OCSF home for the gateway name

Zscaler URL category (Zscaler taxonomy) -> url.categories (string preserved), but OCSF's typed url.category_ids enum uses a different taxonomy, so a category-id pivot loses it [URL-category taxonomy seam]
Zscaler URL class (a second taxonomy level, e.g. Bandwidth Loss); OCSF url has one category concept, so the class level has no home [URL-category taxonomy seam]
Zscaler URL super-category (third taxonomy level); no OCSF home [URL-category taxonomy seam]
cloud-app class/category (CASB taxonomy); OCSF HTTP Activity has no application-taxonomy attribute [app taxonomy]
policy reason (the action taken + the policy applied, a composite) -> status_detail free string; the rule reference and the action collapse into one detail string
client public (egress/NAT) IP; src_endpoint.ip already holds the internal client and there is no second-address slot for the egress [pre/post-NAT collapse]
Zscaler malware category (Zscaler taxonomy); no confirmed OCSF malware-category home on HTTP Activity [threat taxonomy]
Zscaler malware super-category; no OCSF home [threat taxonomy]
Zscaler Page Risk Index (0-100) is the destination URL's risk rating -> risk_score, which OCSF defines as the EVENT's risk; the semantics shift (page risk vs event risk)
DLP dictionaries matched; OCSF HTTP Activity has no DLP attributes (a DLP finding is a different class) [signal OCSF HTTP Activity lacks]
DLP engine matched; no OCSF HTTP Activity home [signal OCSF HTTP Activity lacks]

Zscaler file-type (a content classification, distinct from the MIME content_type already mapped); OCSF file.type_id is a filesystem-object enum, not a content taxonomy, so there is no faithful home [file taxonomy]
Zscaler file class (Zscaler taxonomy); no OCSF home [file taxonomy]
transaction protocol (HTTP/HTTPS) -> app_protocol_name (it is the application protocol, not the IP protocol)

detections.name: Anonymizing-proxy / Tor login
detections.desc: flag a login from a known anonymizing proxy

detections.name: Impossible travel
detections.desc: two logins too far apart in space for the time between them

detections.name: MFA fatigue / push bombing
detections.desc: repeated MFA challenges of a specific factor type against one user

detections.name: ThreatInsight / risk-based signal
detections.desc: act on Okta's risk and threat signals for a sign-in

detections.name: Suspicious ASN
detections.desc: match the source autonomous system against a threat-intel ASN list

detections.name: True source IP behind proxy
detections.desc: recover the real client IP and per-hop geo from the forwarding chain

detections.name: ATT&CK technique hunt
detections.desc: pivot on a specific ATT&CK technique id across detections

detections.name: Multi-stage process lineage
detections.desc: reconstruct grandparent->parent->process chains for a detection

detections.name: IOC pivot on indicator type
detections.desc: search by indicator type and value across detections

detections.name: Response-action audit
detections.desc: audit exactly which actions Falcon took on a detection

detections.name: Known-bad hash
detections.desc: match the executable hash against a reputation feed

detections.name: NAT-aware true-source attribution
detections.desc: tie a post-NAT flow back to the real internal host and port

detections.name: App-ID evasion / tunneled-app
detections.desc: flag an app tunneling another app or using an evasive technology

detections.name: Shadow-IT unsanctioned SaaS
detections.desc: flag use of an unsanctioned SaaS application

detections.name: Firewall action / session-teardown audit
detections.desc: distinguish drop vs reset vs deny and why a session ended

detections.name: Long-lived high-volume beacon
detections.desc: long-duration flow with directional byte/packet asymmetry to a destination

detections.name: NAT-aware true-source attribution (ASA)
detections.desc: tie a NAT-translated flow back to the real internal host and port

detections.name: Connection-lifecycle correlation
detections.desc: pair a Built with its Teardown to reason over the full connection

detections.name: Session-teardown reason analysis
detections.desc: classify why connections ended (reset vs fin vs timeout)

detections.name: ICMP tunneling / covert channel
detections.desc: detect anomalous ICMP type/code patterns

detections.name: Byte-volume exfil by destination
detections.desc: high total bytes to a destination over a single connection

detections.name: DNS tunneling by query characteristics
detections.desc: flag tunneling via record type, domain length/entropy and rcode

detections.name: Identity-attributed DNS risk
detections.desc: attribute a malicious-domain lookup to the specific user or device

detections.name: Content-category / blocked-category policy
detections.desc: analyze blocks by Umbrella content category

detections.name: Egress-IP correlation
detections.desc: correlate DNS activity by external egress / NAT IP

detections.name: Blocked-domain hunt
detections.desc: find blocked resolutions of a specific domain

detections.name: URL-category exfil / policy evasion
detections.desc: data egress to a risky URL category across the category hierarchy

detections.name: Cloud-app shadow-IT (CASB)
detections.desc: flag risky or unsanctioned cloud-app usage by app class

detections.name: DLP incident triage
detections.desc: investigate a DLP match: which dictionary/engine fired, on which URL

detections.name: Malware download by file type
detections.desc: flag a malicious download of a given file type/class

detections.name: HTTP error-rate / response monitoring
detections.desc: monitor response codes and sizes per host and method
